raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California law currently fails to adequately protect victims of stalking by not recognizing threats to pets as a component of threatening behavior.",
    "One in three women (31.2%) and one in six men (16.1%) in the U.S. have experienced stalking in their lifetime according to the National Intimate Partner and Sexual Violence Survey.",
    "Four in ten stalkers threaten not only their victims but also their victims' family members, friends, co-workers, or pets, with over 87,000 cases of threats against pets reported according to the National Crime Victimization Survey.",
    "The standard of proof to convict a stalker is the highest of all standards in law, requiring proof beyond a reasonable doubt of a pattern of stalking behavior.",
    "SB 221 amends Penal Code Sec. 646.9 to align with the federal anti-stalking statute by including threats to a victim’s pet as a qualifying act of intimidation."
  ]
}
```
